headline: Meta Acquires AI-Agent Social Network, Signaling New Platform Battleground
summary: Meta has agreed to buy Moltbook, a startup building social infrastructure specifically for autonomous AI agents to interact with each other. This signals that major platforms are moving to own the social graph for AI systems — not just humans. The strategic implication: whoever controls how AI agents discover, communicate, and transact with each other will have significant leverage over the next generation of application ecosystems.
oci_implication: As OCI builds out its AI infrastructure and agent-hosting capabilities, this move suggests platform lock-in will extend to agent-to-agent networking — OCI should consider what role it plays in enabling or competing with these emerging agent coordination layers.
